var_functions.query_db:42.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

result.preview: {"description": "Located at 12337 Olive Blvd in Creve Coeur, MO, this establishment offers a range of services in Nail Salons, Beauty & Spas.", "regex_results": [{"pattern": ",\\s*([A-Z]{2})\b", "matches": []}, {"pattern": ", ([A-Z]{2}),", "matches": ["MO"]}, {"pattern": "([A-Z]{2}),", "matches": ["MO"]}, {"pattern": "([A-Z]{2})\b", "matches": []}]}

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load all businesses with WiFi attribute
file_path = 'file_storage/functions.query_db:70.json'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Debug the regex pattern specifically
first_business = businesses[2]  # This one has WiFi='free'
desc = first_business.get('description', '')

# Test different regex approaches
patterns = [
    r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b',
    r', ([A-Z]{2}),',
    r'([A-Z]{2}),',
    r'([A-Z]{2})\b',
]

regex_results = []
for pattern in patterns:
    matches = re.findall(pattern, desc)
    regex_results.append({
        'pattern': pattern,
        'matches': matches
    })

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'description': desc,
    'regex_results': regex_results
}))

var_functions.query_db:42.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Load all businesses with WiFi attribute
file_path = 'file_storage/functions.query_db:70.json'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Debug the regex pattern specifically
first_business = businesses[2]  # This one has WiFi='free'
desc = first_business.get('description', '')

# Test different regex approaches
patterns = [
    r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b',
    r', ([A-Z]{2}),',
    r'([A-Z]{2}),',
    r'([A-Z]{2})\b',
]

regex_results = []
for pattern in patterns:
    matches = re.findall(pattern, desc)
    regex_results.append({
        'pattern': pattern,
        'matches': matches
    })

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'description': desc,
    'regex_results': regex_results
}))

var_functions.query_db:42.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

result.preview: {"wifi_by_state": {"MO": 2, "FL": 5, "LA": 1, "IL": 1, "PA": 8, "NV": 1, "IN": 2, "ID": 1, "AB": 1}, "top_state": ["PA", 8], "total_wifi_businesses": 22, "all_states_found": ["MO", "FL", "LA", "IL", "PA", "NV", "IN", "ID", "AB"]}

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load all businesses with WiFi attribute
file_path = 'file_storage/functions.query_db:70.json'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Count WiFi businesses by state
wifi_by_state = {}
business_ids_with_wifi = []

# Use a working pattern: r', ([A-Z]{2}),'
state_pattern = r', ([A-Z]{2}),'

for business in businesses:
    # Extract state from description
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    state_match = re.search(state_pattern, desc)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else None
    
    # Check if WiFi is actually offered (free or paid)
    attributes = business.get('attributes', {})
    if attributes and isinstance(attributes, dict):
        wifi = attributes.get('WiFi', '')
        wifi_str = str(wifi)
        
        # Check for actual WiFi offerings (free or paid), not 'no'
        has_wifi = False
        if 'free' in wifi_str.lower() or 'paid' in wifi_str.lower():
            if 'no' not in wifi_str.lower():
                has_wifi = True
        
        if has_wifi and state:
            business_ids_with_wifi.append(business.get('business_id'))
            wifi_by_state[state] = wifi_by_state.get(state, 0) + 1
            
            # Debug first few
            if len(business_ids_with_wifi) <= 5:
                print(f"DEBUG: {business.get('business_id')} in {state} has {wifi_str}")

# Find state with most WiFi businesses
top_state = max(wifi_by_state.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]) if wifi_by_state else None

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'wifi_by_state': wifi_by_state,
    'top_state': top_state,
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(business_ids_with_wifi),
    'all_states_found': list(wifi_by_state.keys())
}))

var_functions.query_db:42.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.execute_python:76.description: Located at 12337 Olive Blvd in Creve Coeur, MO, this establishment offers a range of services in Nail Salons, Beauty & Spas.

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Load all businesses with WiFi attribute
file_path = 'file_storage/functions.query_db:70.json'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Count WiFi businesses by state
wifi_by_state = {}
business_ids_with_wifi = []

# Use a working pattern: r', ([A-Z]{2}),'
state_pattern = r', ([A-Z]{2}),'

for business in businesses:
    # Extract state from description
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    state_match = re.search(state_pattern, desc)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else None
    
    # Check if WiFi is actually offered (free or paid)
    attributes = business.get('attributes', {})
    if attributes and isinstance(attributes, dict):
        wifi = attributes.get('WiFi', '')
        wifi_str = str(wifi)
        
        # Check for actual WiFi offerings (free or paid), not 'no'
        has_wifi = False
        if 'free' in wifi_str.lower() or 'paid' in wifi_str.lower():
            if 'no' not in wifi_str.lower():
                has_wifi = True
        
        if has_wifi and state:
            business_ids_with_wifi.append(business.get('business_id'))
            wifi_by_state[state] = wifi_by_state.get(state, 0) + 1
            
            # Debug first few
            if len(business_ids_with_wifi) <= 5:
                print(f"DEBUG: {business.get('business_id')} in {state} has {wifi_str}")

# Find state with most WiFi businesses
top_state = max(wifi_by_state.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]) if wifi_by_state else None

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'wifi_by_state': wifi_by_state,
    'top_state': top_state,
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(business_ids_with_wifi),
    'all_states_found': list(wifi_by_state.keys())
}))

var_functions.query_db:42.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

result.preview: {"pa_wifi_business_ids": ["businessref_89", "businessref_97", "businessref_67", "businessref_77", "businessref_86", "businessref_40", "businessref_44", "businessref_82"], "count": 8}

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load all businesses with WiFi attribute
file_path = 'file_storage/functions.query_db:70.json'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Find WiFi businesses in PA (top state)
pa_wifi_business_ids = []
state_pattern = r', ([A-Z]{2}),'

for business in businesses:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    state_match = re.search(state_pattern, desc)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else None
    
    attributes = business.get('attributes', {})
    if attributes and isinstance(attributes, dict):
        wifi = attributes.get('WiFi', '')
        wifi_str = str(wifi)
        
        has_wifi = False
        if 'free' in wifi_str.lower() or 'paid' in wifi_str.lower():
            if 'no' not in wifi_str.lower():
                has_wifi = True
        
        if has_wifi and state == 'PA':
            business_id = business.get('business_id')
            # Convert to business_ref format (businessid_X -> businessref_X)
            business_ref_id = business_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
            pa_wifi_business_ids.append(business_ref_id)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'pa_wifi_business_ids': pa_wifi_business_ids,
    'count': len(pa_wifi_business_ids)
}))

var_functions.query_db:42.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.execute_python:76.description: Located at 12337 Olive Blvd in Creve Coeur, MO, this establishment offers a range of services in Nail Salons, Beauty & Spas.

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Load all businesses with WiFi attribute
file_path = 'file_storage/functions.query_db:70.json'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Find WiFi businesses in PA (top state)
pa_wifi_business_ids = []
state_pattern = r', ([A-Z]{2}),'

for business in businesses:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    state_match = re.search(state_pattern, desc)
    state = state_match.group(1) if state_match else None
    
    attributes = business.get('attributes', {})
    if attributes and isinstance(attributes, dict):
        wifi = attributes.get('WiFi', '')
        wifi_str = str(wifi)
        
        has_wifi = False
        if 'free' in wifi_str.lower() or 'paid' in wifi_str.lower():
            if 'no' not in wifi_str.lower():
                has_wifi = True
        
        if has_wifi and state == 'PA':
            business_id = business.get('business_id')
            # Convert to business_ref format (businessid_X -> businessref_X)
            business_ref_id = business_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
            pa_wifi_business_ids.append(business_ref_id)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'pa_wifi_business_ids': pa_wifi_business_ids,
    'count': len(pa_wifi_business_ids)
}))

var_functions.query_db:42.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

exec_args.sql: SELECT business_ref, rating FROM review WHERE business_ref IN ('businessref_89', 'businessref_97', 'businessref_67', 'businessref_77', 'businessref_86', 'businessref_40', 'businessref_44', 'businessref_82')

result.preview: {"total_reviews": 281, "average_rating": 3.48, "ratings_sample": [5, 4, 4, 4, 1, 5, 5, 5, 5, 2]}

args.code:
import json

# Load reviews for PA WiFi businesses
file_path = 'file_storage/functions.query_db:82.json'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Extract ratings
ratings = []
for review in reviews:
    rating = int(review.get('rating', 0))
    ratings.append(rating)

# Calculate average rating
if ratings:
    avg_rating = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
else:
    avg_rating = 0

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_reviews': len(ratings),
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2),
    'ratings_sample': ratings[:10]
}))

var_functions.query_db:42.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.execute_python:76.description: Located at 12337 Olive Blvd in Creve Coeur, MO, this establishment offers a range of services in Nail Salons, Beauty & Spas.

val_args.code:
import json

# Load reviews for PA WiFi businesses
file_path = 'file_storage/functions.query_db:82.json'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Extract ratings
ratings = []
for review in reviews:
    rating = int(review.get('rating', 0))
    ratings.append(rating)

# Calculate average rating
if ratings:
    avg_rating = sum(ratings) / len(ratings)
else:
    avg_rating = 0

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_reviews': len(ratings),
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2),
    'ratings_sample': ratings[:10]
}))

var_functions.query_db:42.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened
